reasoning: The resolution requires Nancy Pelosi's Quiver Quantitative portfolio return to exceed the S&P 500's return from the end of 2024 to the end of 2025. As of mid-October 2025, Pelosi was trailing by over 5.5% (+7.14% vs S&P's +12.7%). As of early December 2025, the S&P 500 has continued to rally significantly to around 6,904, putting its YTD return around 17.4%. Assuming Pelosi's portfolio (which has an estimated historical beta of 1.14) moved roughly in line with the market, it is still likely trailing the S&P 500 by 4-6%. With only three weeks left in the year, closing a substantial return gap requires significant idiosyncratic outperformance (alpha) from her concentrated tech-heavy holdings. The community estimated a 37% probability on Nov 27; since then, 10 more days have elapsed, further reducing the time remaining to close the deficit. Absent an enormous December surge isolated to her largest specific holdings (e.g., heavily tilted ITM tech options), time decay makes it increasingly difficult to catch up, leading me to lower the probability to 28%.
